page: Amazon Web Services r6id.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor. This server features 32 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data places this instance within the average performance tier for most standard compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1892.59 and a multi-core score of 30966.98, while the PassMark CPU score is recorded at 36684.51. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1657.98 GB/sec and an uncached large block write bandwidth of 67.57 GB/sec. For database-specific workloads, the server handles 31.0 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 14858.19 on PassMark database operations.

While the instance demonstrates capable performance for small to medium language model tasks, such as processing 2193.17 tokens per second on a 135M parameter model, it exhibits significant performance tradeoffs with larger models. For a 70B parameter model, prompt processing drops to 5.12 tokens per second and text generation falls to 2.26 tokens per second, placing it in the bottom 10% tier. Given the high memory capacity, local NVMe storage, and balanced CPU performance, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and medium-scale data processing rather than large-scale AI model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server delivers a balanced hardware profile with 8.0 GB of RAM per core and a 12 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark results place the instance in the middle 50% tier for general compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations, achieving 31.0 million Redis SET operations per second. However, it exhibits a performance tradeoff in large-scale AI workloads, with poor inference speeds on 70B parameter models. This instance is designed for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and caching systems.

256.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
